title: QA: (String, bool) tuple return type hides error semantics across tool API
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
naming
## Location
src/tools/bash/mod.rs:319, src/tools/bash/kill.rs:7, src/tools/bash/output.rs:8, src/tools/lsp.rs:73
## Violation
Every public tool entry point returns `(String, bool)` where the `bool` silently means "is_error". This is a primitive-obsession + boolean-flag-return anti-pattern. Callers must read documentation to know which slot is which; a refactor that inverts the bool would compile silently and break runtime behavior.

Quoted (lsp.rs:72-73):
```
#[must_use]
pub fn execute_lsp<S: BuildHasher>(args: &HashMap<String, Value, S>) -> (String, bool) {
```

Quoted (bash/mod.rs:319):
```
pub fn execute_bash(args: &HashMap<String, Value>) -> (String, bool) {
```

Same shape in `execute_kill_shell` (kill.rs:7) and `execute_bash_output` (output.rs:8).
## Mandated refactor
1. Define `pub struct ToolOutput { pub content: String, pub is_error: bool }` with named fields.
2. Or use `Result<String, ToolError>` and have the dispatcher translate to the wire shape.
3. Update every call site in one PR; the type system will surface every miss.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
